tests.intent: Required AWS and managed ORDS inputs remain environment-only and absent from commands, logs, reports and retained evidence; missing, placeholder, unsafe or printed values stop live execution as nonzero NOT RUN.

Live AWS identity, bucket region, object HTTPS request and S3 response observations bind the run to one real S3 REST origin using only hashed identifiers; local, website-HTTP, CloudFront, proxy, replay and dry-run substitutes fail.

The uploaded object inventory equals the build manifest and reviewed extension allowlist byte-for-byte, contains only compiled client production files, and rejects source maps, tools, evaluators, fixtures, reports, goldens, WAD content and secrets.

Every live S3 object has the reviewed deterministic content type and cache policy: index always revalidates, mutable names revalidate, and year-long immutable caching is used only when a filename content digest matches uploaded bytes.

The attested managed ORDS origin is embedded at build time in reviewed compiled bytes with no unresolved marker, runtime config fetch, query override, local fallback, same-origin API assumption, reverse proxy, service worker or alternate dynamic base.

Pinned Playwright and Chromium navigate directly to the explicit live S3 HTTPS index object with one worker, no retries, mocks, route fulfillment, proxy, service worker, skipped tests, console/page errors, failed requests or missing stable ids.

A real browser OPTIONS request from the exact S3 origin reaches the exact managed ORDS origin and proves status, Origin, requested method and headers, allow-origin, allow-methods and allow-headers without wildcard credentials or intercepted response.

Actual cross-origin calls create an unpredictable opaque game token, execute ordered STEP commands, fetch PLAYPAL and audio assets, validate gzip/BLOB/RLE transport and state progression, and never synthesize responses or gameplay in the client.

Raw 320 by 200 canvas RGBA hashes come from database frame payloads and database-authored palette values, while user-gesture audio fetch, decode and scheduling consume server tuples exactly once with no hidden static or third-party media.

The live browser creates a save after commands, diverges, restores exact state and canvas identity, then replays the recorded command sequence to the same canonical state without local persistence substituting for Oracle state.

The reviewed E1M1 completion smoke executes through repeated real STEP transactions from the S3 client, reaches the database completion/intermission state, retains deterministic command and terminal hashes, and rejects shortcuts or prerecorded completion.

The complete browser network ledger contains only the attested S3 origin for static objects and the attested Oracle managed ORDS origin for dynamic calls, with zero redirects, websocket, beacon, analytics, fonts, CDNs, telemetry or unclassified requests.

Canonical secret-free evidence binds build, upload, live object, browser report, CORS, workflow and network observations to one run and frozen ancestry; partial writes, stale files, mutation survivors, timeouts, skips and infrastructure errors fail closed.
